req.title: Hr-Admin updates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-022 covers four distinct risk areas: (1) happy-path update by an authenticated hr-admin with all mandatory fields present (EP – valid partition), (2) submission with a mandatory field blank triggering a 400 validation error (NEG/DT), (3) boundary length on a text field such as 'comments' at exactly the maximum allowed character count (BVA), (4) state transition from 'draft' to 'submitted' to verify the review lifecycle and audit write (STC), and (5) unauthenticated access producing 401 and forbidden role producing 403 (NEG). Together these provide positive, boundary, and negative coverage as required. Each test targets /performance-reviews via Playwright for UI interaction and a lightweight REST call to verify the audit record, keeping each executable within 40 lines.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_all_valid_fields
preconditions: At least one existing performance-review record exists with known id
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Verify success response and entity id in UI
Call audit API to confirm audit record was written
expected: Form submits without validation errors
UI shows success confirmation with entity id
HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Audit record exists for the updated review id
trace: R-WEB-022: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_all_valid_fields(
    hr_admin_page,       # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page
    existing_review_id,  # <fixture>: str, seeded review id
    audit_api_client,    # <fixture>: requests.Session for audit service
    valid_review_payload # <fixture>: dict of valid mandatory field values
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}/edit")

    <helper_fn>.fill_review_form(page, valid_review_payload)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response body missing entity id"

    expect(page.get_by_role("alert")).to_contain_text("saved")

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get(
        f"/audit-records?entity_id={body['id']}&action=UPDATE"
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json().get("records", [])
    assert len(records) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_review_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: At least one existing performance-review record exists with known id
actions: Clear the mandatory 'reviewer_name' field, leave it blank
Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Form submission is blocked or returns HTTP 400
Inline validation error message is displayed for 'reviewer_name'
No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-022: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_review_comments_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: Max allowed length for comments field is known (e.g. 2000 chars)
At least one existing performance-review record exists with known id
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Set 'comments' field to exactly MAX_COMMENTS_LENGTH characters
expected: Form accepts comments at exactly the maximum length
HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Updated record persists the full comments text
Audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-022: mandatory fields must be validated (boundary of optional long-text field)
R-WEB-022: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_COMMENTS_LENGTH = 2000

def test_hr_admin_update_review_comments_at_max_boundary_length(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_review_id,
    audit_api_client,
    valid_review_payload
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    boundary_comments = "A" * MAX_COMMENTS_LENGTH
    payload = {**valid_review_payload, "comments": boundary_comments}

    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}/edit")
    <helper_fn>.fill_review_form(page, payload)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    saved_comments = body.get("comments", "")
    assert len(saved_comments) == MAX_COMMENTS_LENGTH, "Comments length mismatch"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get(
        f"/audit-records?entity_id={body['id']}&action=UPDATE"
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json().get("records", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_access_performance_review_edit
actions: Without logging in, navigate directly to /performance-reviews/{review_id}/edit
expected: User is redirected to login page OR receives HTTP 401
Edit form is not accessible
No audit record is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_access_performance_review_edit(
    browser_context_factory,  # <fixture>: factory for a fresh, unauthenticated context
    existing_review_id,
    base_url                  # <fixture>: str, e.g. "https://hr-app.example.com"
):
    context = browser_context_factory()  # no stored auth state
    page = context.new_page()

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r))

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}/edit")

    api_calls = [
        r for r in responses
        if "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("GET", "PUT", "POST")
    ]

    unauthorized = any(r.status == 401 for r in api_calls)
    redirected_to_login = "login" in page.url or "signin" in page.url

    assert unauthorized or redirected_to_login, (
        f"Expected 401 or login redirect; got url={page.url}, "
        f"statuses={[r.status for r in api_calls]}"
    )

    context.close()

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_performance_review
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role 'employee'
Attempt to submit the update form (or observe page access)
expected: Access is denied with HTTP 403 or UI shows 'Forbidden'
Form is not rendered or save action is rejected
No audit record of a successful update is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_performance_review(
    employee_page,       # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for role:employee
    existing_review_id,
    valid_review_payload,
    audit_api_client
):
    page = employee_page
    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r))

    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}/edit")

    nav_calls = [
        r for r in responses if "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method == "GET"
    ]
    if any(r.status == 403 for r in nav_calls):
        return  # access denied at navigation level – pass

    # If page loaded, attempt save and expect 403
    if page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").is_visible():
        <helper_fn>.fill_review_form(page, valid_review_payload)
        with page.expect_response(
            lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")
        ) as resp_info:
            page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()
        assert resp_info.value.status == 403, "Expected 403 for forbidden role"
    else:
        expect(page.get_by_text("Forbidden")).to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_review_state_transition_draft_to_submitted_writes_audit
preconditions: A performance-review record exists in 'draft' state with known id
actions: Verify new state persisted in response
Verify audit record reflects state change
expected: Review state transitions from 'draft' to 'submitted'
HTTP response 200 or 201 with updated entity
Audit record captures previous state 'draft' and new state 'submitted'
trace: R-WEB-022: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_review_state_transition_draft_to_submitted_writes_audit(
    hr_admin_page,
    draft_review_id,    # <fixture>: review id seeded in 'draft' state
    audit_api_client,
    valid_review_payload
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"/performance-reviews/{draft_review_id}/edit")

    <helper_fn>.fill_review_form(page, valid_review_payload)
    page.get_by_label("Status").select_option("submitted")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/performance-reviews" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201)

    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "submitted", f"State not transitioned: {body.get('status')}"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get(
        f"/audit-records?entity_id={body['id']}&action=STATE_CHANGE"
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json().get("records", [])
    assert any(
        r.get("previous_state") == "draft" and r.get("new_state") == "submitted"
        for r in records
    ), "Audit record missing state transition details"
